signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Conflict + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.87 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite US-Iran military exchanges in the Middle East. While Middle East war fuels cost pressures (Japan Services PMI at 11-month low of 51.0), the carry advantage remains dominant until VIX exceeds 25. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) confirms crowded carry positioning. Intervention risk remains acute on any rapid move toward 160.00 following May holiday activity.

signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hike Bias
signals.text: NZD/CAD at 0.4355 — NZD outperformance (+0.581% avg) vs CAD weakness (-0.599% avg) generates a 1.180% G8 spread. NZD strength is supported by the RBNZ hike bias and COT LF net short of -16,833 (23.0% long), creating a short-squeeze environment as specs fade the hawkish CB. CAD remains the G8 laggard as oil price volatility persists despite the 150bp USD/CAD carry advantage.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Trade Policy + Correlation Break
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1738 — Trump's July 4 deadline for EU trade compliance introduces structural downside risk despite the US court striking down 10% global tariffs. EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.715 (z=+1.92σ) indicates a significant break from the -0.141 norm; the pair is currently tracking equity sentiment rather than the 175bp Fed-ECB carry deficit. COT LF net short -18,293 suggests specs are already positioned for Eurozone headwinds.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7224 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 83% short creates a sharp institutional vs retail divergence. While the 60bp AUD carry premium (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%) supports the long-term bid, the extreme retail short positioning suggests a risk of a 'short-squeeze' extension if price holds above 0.7200. RBA hiking stance remains the primary fundamental anchor.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Carry + Retail Extreme
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7799 — 375bp Fed-SNB carry advantage remains the widest in the G10 space, supporting the USD bid. Retail sentiment is at 82% long, aligning with the carry direction but signaling late-cycle crowding. COT LF net short -6,117 (33.6% long) shows institutional specs are less committed than retail. Carry remains the dominant regime driver while VIX stays in 'normal' territory at 17.1.
